# bitbucketdc_project_access_key (Resource)

Manages a single SSH access key for a Bitbucket Data Center project. Each resource represents one key granting read or write access to all repositories in the project.

## Example Usage

```terraform
resource "bitbucketdc_project_access_key" "ci" {
  project_key = "PLAT"
  public_key  = "ssh-ed25519 AAAAC3NzaC1lZDI1NTE5AAAAI... ci-deploy-key"
  label       = "CI Deploy Key"
  permission  = "PROJECT_READ"
}
```

<!-- schema generated by tfplugindocs -->
## Schema

### Required

- `permission` (String) The permission level granted to the key: PROJECT_READ or PROJECT_WRITE.
- `project_key` (String) The key of the project to add the SSH access key to. Forces replacement if changed.
- `public_key` (String) The SSH public key text (e.g. 'ssh-rsa AAAA...'). Forces replacement if changed.

### Optional

- `label` (String) A human-readable label for the key. Bitbucket may derive a label from the SSH key comment when none is supplied. Forces replacement if changed because Bitbucket does not provide an update endpoint for labels.

### Read-Only

- `fingerprint` (String) The SSH key fingerprint (computed by Bitbucket).
- `id` (String) Unique identifier in the format {project_key}/{key_id}.
- `key_id` (Number) The server-assigned numeric identifier of the SSH key.
